learning_outcomes.description: understand the functional and situational nature of writing in the professional workplace
be familiar with the conventions, styles and formats of the most common professional communications
be able to generate, evaluate, analyse, synthesise, and organise information, using problem-solving strategies
have the skills to create specific professional documents that are accessible to a wide range of readers
understand how professional writing and editing works and be able to take responsibility for your ongoing development as writer/editors in the professions for which you are preparing.

assessments.weighting: <dd>15% This assessment is in two parts, each marked Pass/Fail. </dd>
assessments.task_description:
You'll collaborate with a small group of classmates to produce minutes for one of your tutorial meetings and present the minutes for class endorsement in the next meeting.

This assessment is in two parts, both assessed pass/fail: the team-authored minutes (10%) and an individually-authored team evaluation (5%).

Signups will happen in the Week 4 tutorial meeting, when your team will develop your team charter together. The first set of minutes will be taken in the Week 5 tutorial meeting (presented in Week 6), and the last set in Week 12 (presented in Week 13).

Preparing for and participating in your group is part of the requirements for this task. If you fail to enrol in a group or if you do not participate adequately in your agreed group, you cannot be added to a/another group at a later stage as this would disadvantage other team members. In this scenario, you will earn a 0 for this assessment item.

Full details, including the marking criteria and standards, are in the Assessments folder on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: In-class activity, with written materials submitted via Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment. Failure to attend the relevant tutorials and complete the activity will result in a zero mark for this task.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You'll write a summary of a peer-reviewed research article for a lay audience. You will also complete a reflection which analyses your research and writing process, and the piece of writing you produced.

Full details, including the marking criteria and standards, are in the Assessments folder on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
27/10/2026 - 28/10/2026

Your submission is due by the end of your assigned tutorial meeting.
assessments.task_description:
You'll write a statement explaining how your work in this course, including your participation in tutorials and your written submissions, has equipped you to meet specified selection criteria.

You'll write and submit the statement during your Week 13 tutorial meeting (uploading typed text or a scan of handwritten text to Blackboard).

Full details, including the marking criteria and standards, are in the Assessments folder on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment. Failure to attend the assigned tutorial, complete the activity and upload it before the end of your assigned tutorial meeting will result in a zero mark.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
You'll write a report of no more than 1500 words, making a recommendation to your manager about one of a number of specified scenarios.

Full details, including briefing documents with background information on the scenarios, as well as the marking criteria and standards, are in the Assessments folder on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
